body.thing: escalation can leave the local box
body.intention: A generalist request escalated to the top capability rung resolves to a model on a non-owned_local source, instead of resolving to nothing and raising a capability-ceiling alarm.

red_run.summary:
top = bump_difficulty("code", 2)
        decision = engine.resolve(
            RouteRequest(ticket_tier="builder", builder_tier="builder", domain=""),
            required_difficulty=top,
        )
>       assert decision is not None, (
            f"a generalist request at the top capability rung ({top!r}) resolves to nothing — "
            f"escalation halts with a capability-ceiling alarm while more capable models sit on "
            f"the rack, unreachable"
        )
E       AssertionError: a generalist request at the top capability rung ('frontier') resolves to nothing — escalation halts with a capability-ceiling alarm while more capable models sit on the rack, unreachable
E       assert None is not None

tests/inference/test_escalation_leaves_the_local_box.py:203: AssertionError
------------------------------ Captured log call -------------------------------
WARNING  unseen_university.system_alarms:system_alarms.py:242 SYSTEM_ALARM|signature=inference-no-capable-connection:generalist:frontier|caller=inference.rules_engine.resolve|count=2|fatal=False|resolve: no capable connection for domain=generalist at difficulty>=frontier (ticket_tier=builder, builder_tier=builder, urgency=normal) — escalated past available capability, halting for analysis
WARNING  unseen_university.devices.inference.rules_engine:rules_engine.py:247 rules: resolve no capable connection → system_alarm (domain=generalist difficulty=frontier)
=========================== short test summary info ============================
FAILED tests/inference/test_escalation_leaves_the_local_box.py::test_the_real_rack_can_escalate_off_the_local_box
1 failed in 0.18s

body.why: Measured: across all dimension combinations the resolver reached 3 of 22 models, all on ollama. cost_class sorts first and difficulty_meets is >=, so any local model dominates every bucket it CLAIMS — and with three buckets the box held one of each. The fix is not to abandon cost-first (the cheap local model still wins every rung it can serve) but to give capability a rung the box does not hold, and to require MEASURED evidence to claim it, because a cost-first selector rewards overclaiming.
